ocr.text: In evaluating the actuarial reserve movements and policyholder dividend allocations within the AXA Group's North American
segment, it is essential to analyze the evolving metrics that impact our financial stability and policyholder satisfaction.
ActuarialRatios  ReserveRatio  LossRatio  ExpenseRatios  CombinedRatio
CAGR(5Years)  NetIncome  ClaimsPaid  UnderwritingProfit  InvestmentIncome
2023  2022  2021  2020  2019  Average  GrowthRate
The data table presented herein highlights critical ratios, including the policyholder dividend allocation, which reflects our
commitment to returning value to our clients. For instance, the reserve ratio under the "100.5%" column was recorded at 90.0%,
indicating a robust financial position. Furthermore, the allocation metrics show that the "95.3%" category stood at 94.0%,
demonstrating effective management of reserves relative to policy liabilities. The movement trends for individual policies are
particularly noteworthy; the total dividends allocated to these policies reached significant figures, with our GroupPolicies
reporting a TotalDividends figure of TotalDividends.
PolicyholderSegment  TotalDividends  DividendRate(%)  ClaimPayments  ReservesAllocated  RegionalBreakdown
IndividualPolicies  $50M  5.2%  $20M  $10M  NorthAmerica  TotalDividends
[HIDDEN]  $30M  4.7%  $15M  $8M  NorthAmerica  [HIDDEN]
CommercialPolicies  $40M  6.1%  $25M  $12M  NorthAmerica  TotalDividends
LifeInsurance  $70M  5.8%  $30M  $15M  NorthAmerica  TotalDividends
P&CInsurance  $60M  [HIDDEN]  $35M  $20M  NorthAmerica  TotalDividends
HealthInsurance  $20M  [HIDDEN]  $10M  $5M  NorthAmerica  TotalDividends
Additionally, in our examination of the various insurance sectors, the P&CInsurance line experienced a notable allocation
percentage of 6.3% under the significant $50M threshold, reinforcing our strategic emphasis on this sector. As we continue to
navigate a competitive landscape, the emphasis on maintaining strong reserves—such as the 85.0% category at 105.0%—will
be paramount in ensuring we meet our obligations to policyholders while sustaining growth in our market share.
